MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CAGNEY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar per request of the moving party. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2017 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Ottinger, Esq. 415-463-1283 of Ottinger Employment Lawyers for Plaintiff/Moving party.
John Baum, Esq. 415-835-9006 of Hirschfeld Kraemer for Defendant/opposing party.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHARLES BRANDON's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena is Denied. No sanctions.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. Proposed order to include the list of limitations, the due date of documents and must be in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Counsel to submit the proposed order directly to the Judge Pro Tem at drobbins@woodrobbins.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)
